full_text_english: I can't stop watching this.30 seconds of Seinfeld. George Costanza. 100% AI.The tool behind it: LTX 2.3 from Lightricks.This model just came out. And it's already producing footage that looks like it belongs on Netflix.Lip-synced dialogue. 4K resolution. 50 frames per second. Audio generated in a single pass.Here's the process of creating this kind of project:1- LTX 2.3 for video and audio generation2- LTX Desktop to run everything locally3- A text prompt describing the scene4- Topaz or similar for upscaling (optional)Sora costs OpenAI $1M/day (!!!) and is shutting down April 26. But this model is open source and runs on your laptop.Could you tell it was AI-made?

hook_text_english: How to make viral cheatsheets with AI in 5 mins: (even if you have zero design skills)1. Find a stupidly viral YouTube video in your niche.2. Get the transcript. Copy it.3. Open Claude. Click Customiz
full_text_english: How to make viral cheatsheets with AI in 5 mins: (even if you have zero design skills)1. Find a stupidly viral YouTube video in your niche.2. Get the transcript. Copy it.3. Open Claude. Click Customize → Connectors.4. Search "Gamma." Connect your account.5. Now paste this prompt into Claude:"Act like an expert Content Strategist and Information Designer. Your goal is to turn this source into a structured infographic brief, then generate it in Gamma.Here's my source: [PASTE TRANSCRIPT]Part 1: The ExtractionDon't summarize. Pull out the meat:→ Core Thesis: The single most important takeaway, one sentence.→ Key Data/Facts: Specific numbers, case studies, hard facts.→ Golden Quotes: 3–5 verbatim lines that hit hard.→ The Framework: If there's a step-by-step process or model, outline it.Part 2: The Infographic StructureOne focused story, top to bottom. No vague titles like 'Summary of [Topic].'→ A catchy headline→ A one-sentence subtitle (who it's for or why it matters)→ 4–6 clearly labeled sections with a short heading + 1–2 data points each→ A closing takeaway or CTANow send this to Gamma as a Graphic infographic. Use minimal text."6. Claude extracts, structures & sends it to Gamma.7. No copy-pasting between tabs.8. Click the Gamma link Claude gives you.9. Pick your illustration style. Edit it. Done.You just turned a 20-minute video into an infographic you can edit, with AI images.Do it for the videos you've been 'saving for later'.

hook_text_english: 26 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.——/CLEAR clears conversation history and resets context./COST shows token usage fo
full_text_english: 26 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.——/CLEAR clears conversation history and resets context./COST shows token usage for the current session./COMPACT [instructions] compresses the conversation and lets you specify what to keep./RESUME [session] reopens a previous conversation by ID or name./BRANCH [name] creates a new branch from the current conversation./REWIND rolls the conversation and/or code back to an earlier point./RENAME [name] renames the current session. If no name is given, one is generated./EXPORT [filename] exports the conversation as plain text./MODEL [model] switches to another model, such as Sonnet, Opus, or Haiku./USAGE shows plan usage limits and current rate-limit status./EXTRA-USAGE enables extra usage when standard limits are reached./INIT initializes the project with a CLAUDE .md guide./MEMORY edits CLAUDE .md memory files and controls auto-memory./ADD-DIR <path> adds a working directory for file access during the session./DIFF opens a diff viewer for uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs./SECURITY-REVIEW checks pending changes for security issues./PLAN [description] enters plan mode, optionally starting with a task./PERMISSIONS manages allow, ask, and deny rules for tool access./AGENTS manages agent and sub-agent configurations./SKILLS lists all available skills, built-in and custom./PLUGIN manages Claude Code plugins./RELOAD-PLUGINS reloads active plugins without restarting./MCP manages MCP server connections and OAuth authentication./CONFIG opens settings for theme, model, output style, and preferences./THEME changes the color theme, including light, dark, and accessible options./COLOR [color] sets the prompt bar color for the current session.——After months of testing Claude, I built a single prompt library with every prompt I personally use.To access it, complete these 4 steps:1. Subscribe (for free) → how-to-ai.guide.2. Open my welcome email.3. Hit the automatic reply button inside.4. Receive your prompt library + bonus video.

hook_text_english: You're still doing these 5 tasks manually. Claude Computer does them (without you):1. Finding freelancers (post, messages, follow-ups).2. Monitoring competitor pricing every Monday.3. Organizing your
full_text_english: You're still doing these 5 tasks manually. Claude Computer does them (without you):1. Finding freelancers (post, messages, follow-ups).2. Monitoring competitor pricing every Monday.3. Organizing your desktop & downloads.4. Pulling viral ads from Meta Ads into Sheets.5. Repurposing best YouTube videos into short-formHere's how to set it up in 10 minutes:1. Download the Claude desktop app.2. Upgrade to Pro ($20) or Max ($100).3. Go to Settings → Desktop app.4. Turn on Browser use → Turn on Computer use.5. Deny apps you don't want Claude to touch.6. Click on Dispatch in the left sidebar.7. Scan the QR code with your phone's Claude app.8. Now go to your phone. Open Dispatch. 9. Paste these 5 prompt:Prompt 1: Find a freelancer for me"Go to my Fiverr account on Chrome. Post a job offer for [task]. Then, send a message to the best 10 [job needed] for [task]. Check if I have any unanswered DMs, and follow up until one accepts the [task] for [budget] maximum."Prompt 2: Find viral ads from today."Go to the Meta Ads Library: search the latest viral ads about [topic] in [country] only. Create a Google Sheets with at least 50 links to 50 ads that mention [topic].""Now go to Gemini and recreate the ad. But make it about subscribing to my newsletter "How to AI". Each time you generate it on Gemini, I want you to save them on my Canva account in a new folder "Meta Ads". "Prompt 3: Monitor my competitor's pricing."Go to [competitor 1], [competitor 2], and [competitor 3] on Chrome. For each, find the pricing page and extract all plan names, prices, and key features. Compare them side by side in a Google Sheets. Highlight where my pricing at [your website] is higher or lower. Repeat every Monday and flag changes."Prompt 4: Desktop file organization"Go to my Desktop and Downloads folders. Scan every file and sort them into folders: Screenshots, Receipts, PDFs, Images, Documents, Videos, and Other. Rename each file [YYYY-MM-DD]_[short description].[ext]. For receipts, extract vendor, amount & date into a Google Sheets "Receipt Tracker". Delete duplicates. Give me a before/after summary."Prompt 5: Repurpose my best content."Go to my YouTube channel on Chrome. Find my 10 most viewed videos from the past 6 months. full_text_english: Prompting is the worst way to use Claude.Here's what the top 1% do instead:They set up these 8 files once. Then they barely prompt again.File 1: about-me .md (Your identity)Who you are, your job, your priorities. Claude reads this before every task.To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome emailFile 2: voice-profile .md (Your taste DNA)Your beliefs, your writing mechanics, your hard nos. Built from a 100-question interview with Claude.File 3: anti-ai-writing-style .md (Your boundaries)Every word you ban, the structure you reject, tone you hate. 80% of this file is what you're NOT.Go to how-to-ai.guide to download anti AI guide.Don't pay .Open the email. Click on Notion. Open '.md files' Download 'ANTI AI STYLE .md'. File 4: The Cowork Folder (Your 4-folder system)ABOUT ME. PROJECTS. TEMPLATES. CLAUDE OUTPUTS. 3 read-only, 1 write. Nothing extra.File 5: Global Instructions (Your persistent rules)Set once in Settings → Cowork → EditClaude follows them before every task. Prompt: "Always read my files first, never edit my originals, deliver everything to CLAUDE OUTPUTS." File 6: The One Prompt (How you start every chat)29 words. Forces Claude to ask YOU questions. Starts 80% of your conversations.Prompt: "I want to [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. Use AskUserQuestion before you start." File 7: Connectors (Claude inside your tools)Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Gmail, Figma. No copy-pasting. Claude reads your actual tools.File 8: Plugins (Instant skill packs)Marketing, Sales, Legal, Data. One-click install. Each comes with its own slash commands.The secret was always these 8 files behind it.I wrote 2 guides so you can copy my exact system:✦ My full 8-file setup: how-to-ai.guide✦ My Cowork folder walkthrough: claude-co.work(save this to never write a long prompt to Claude)

hook_text_english: How to use Claude Cowork 4x faster:Step 1. Stop typing. (I'm serious.) Step 2. Go to wispr .ai.Step 3. Download Wispr Flow. It's free.Step 4. Pick your key shortcut. I use Shift.Step 5. Open Claude Co
full_text_english: How to use Claude Cowork 4x faster:Step 1. Stop typing. (I'm serious.) Step 2. Go to wispr .ai.Step 3. Download Wispr Flow. It's free.Step 4. Pick your key shortcut. I use Shift.Step 5. Open Claude Cowork. Hold Shift. Talk.Step 6. Release. Your words appear in the chat box. Step 7. Use this free set up guide: claude-co.workNo integration. No config. No heavy setup. You hold a key, you talk, and it types.✦ Here's why your Cowork sessions are slow:"Let me think about how to word this.""Hold on, I need to type out the context.""I'll just click the default option, close enough.""Let me re-read what I typed before sending."These are killing you. Every session. Every question.Cowork asks you 8 questions. You type each answer in 2 mins. That's 16 mins of you being slow.Spoken? 8 questions. 90 secs. Richer answers.If you type, you self-edit. You delete & rephrase. You kill context before Claude ever sees it.When you talk, you dump your thinking. Raw. Full details that make Cowork's output good.The fix is counterintuitive:→ Stop trying to type better prompts.☑ Talk them. Messy, unfiltered, long. ☑ Cowork figures out what matters.Typed feedback: "Make it less formal."Spoken feedback: "The tone is too stiff. I want it to sound like I'm texting a friend who runs a 200-person company. Keep the data but make it casual. Only redo section 2."Your voice is the context. Stop leaving it out.♻️ Repost to help one person to use Claude faster.

full_text_english: How to climb all 4 layers of Claude in one weekend:(even if you just signed up yesterday)✦ Level 1: Claude Chat (Saturday morning)Go to claude .com/download. Install the app.Pay the $20. Select Opus 4.6 + Extended Thinking. Connect Slack, Drive, Notion through Connectors.Stop writing long prompts. Prompt this instead:"I want to [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. Use AskUserQuestion before you start."Most people stay here forever.You're leaving 90% of Claude untouched.Claude Basics: how-to-claude.ai.——✦ Level 2: Claude Cowork (Saturday afternoon)Go to 'Cowork'. Create a folder "Claude-Cowork."4 subfolders. About me, template, project & outputsCreate about-me .md: what you do, how you do.Create anti-ai-style .md: words you'd never say. Set Global Instructions (Settings → Cowork → Edit): "Always read my files first, never edit my originals, deliver everything to CLAUDE OUTPUTS."You just killed prompting.From now on, your prompt is 2 lines + your folder.Move on to Cowork: claude-co.work——✦ Level 3: Skills + Plugins (Sunday morning)Open Cowork. Type: "Use the skill-creator to help me build a skill for [your most repeated task]."Claude interviews you. Answer and be specific.It generates a SKILL .md. Test it: "When would you use this skill?" If the description is vague, fix it.Upload: Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Upload. Now it fires automatically. No slash command.Install Plugins: Cowork > Customize > Browse pluginSkills with about-me .md. Skill handles the process. Voice file handles tone. Two layers simultaneously.Set up Claude Skills: https://lnkd.in/g9MQUC-Z——✦ Level 4: Code + Computer (Sunday afternoon)Click the Code tab. Create a folder. Connect GitHub (free account → Settings → Connectors).Prompt: "Create a GitHub repo named [project]. Code everything. Don't ask for permissions."Download VS Code. Install the Claude extension. Turn on "Skip Permissions" to go 100x faster.After your session, paste this: "Create a CLAUDE .md file with everything you learned about this project." Now Claude remembers your fonts, colors, and structure forever.Claude code guide: https://lnkd.in/gsRhsFxTClaude Computer: Settings → Desktop app → turn on Browser use + Computer use. Connect your phone with Dispatch. Text a task. Schedule a recurring task: left sidebar → Scheduled → write the prompt → pick the frequency.Claude Computer: https://lnkd.in/dSaSqRxwThis is where you stop working inside Claude.Claude starts working on your computer.——Saturday, you were prompting like it's ChatGPT.Sunday, Claude is running your screen, building your website, & sending you text updates.That's all 4 layers. In one weekend.

You type. You get an answer.✦ Extended Thinking: It reasons deeper.✦ Opus 4.6: The smartest model. Prompt "do like & it knows it.✦ Context Files: Drop everything you know into files✦ about-me .md: A file with what you do & think.✦ Download my files at how-to-guide.ai (for free).✦ Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email.Layer 3: The features that work for you✦ Skills: Long instructions living inside the AI. ✦ Skill Creator: It interviews you to build the Skills.✦ SKILL .md: Trigger file, description & instructions. ✦ Negative Triggers: The "Do NOT use for..." lines. ✦ Claude Skills 101: https://lnkd.in/dq6xHS4p Layer 4: The features that replace your workflow✦ Cowork: It reads, creates, and executes.✦ Cowork Projects: Memory that persists.✦ Global Instructions: Instructions across all chats. ✦ Scheduled Tasks: Claude runs tasks automatically✦ Dispatch: Control Claude from your phone.✦ Claude Cowork 101: claude-co.workLayer 5: The features that connect everything✦ Connectors: Plug into Slack, Calendar & Gmail. ✦ Plugins: Build and upload your own app.✦ Claude Computer: Clicks, browse & operates Mac✦ Claude Code: English is the new code to ship fast✦ AskUserQuestion: Interactive form to prompt you.✦ Gamma: Claude for brief. Gamma for slides.✦ CLAUDE .md: A memory file for Claude Code. ✦ Claude Code 101: https://lnkd.in/d4qamGtMThat's all 25 features. On one page.I turned this into a full visual infographic you can save, print, or send to your team.To download all of my Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.♻️ Repost this to help someone use Claude in 2026.

full_text_english: How to set up Claude Cowork completely in 1 hour:(duplicate my exact folder, files, and prompts)1. Download the Claude desktop app.2. Click the Cowork tab at the top.3. You need a Pro plan ($20/mo). Worth it.-----→ 0-10 min: Build your 'Claude Cowork' folder.Create "CLAUDE COWORK" on your computer.Inside: About me, Projects, Templates, Output files.To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email(The most important step, don't skip it)→ 10-25 min: Write your .md files.about-me .md = who you are, how you work.anti-ai-style .md = every word you'd never use.To download my files, go to how-to-ai.guide.(These two files replace 500-word prompts forever)→ 25-35 min: Set Global Instructions.Go to Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions.Folder rules. Naming conventions.What Claude must read before every task.(You write this once. It runs every time)→ 35-45 min: Create your first Cowork Project.Cowork tab → Projects → +.Pick a task you do every week.Scoped memory = it remember what it did last week(You stop re-explaining yourself)→ 45-55 min: Run your first real task.Prompt: "I want to [task]. Ask me questions first."Claude generates clickable forms to prompt you.It creates a real .docx in your folder.(Stop prompting. Start directing)→ 55-60 min: Schedule a task."Every Monday at 7am, create my weekly briefing."You wake up to a finished doc. That's the endgame.Pro tip: Always add "Use AskUserQuestion" to every prompt. Claude prompts you instead.To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

full_text_english: Your writing still sounds like AI-written text. Here's a secret hack to humanize it:1. Go to how-to-ai.guide to download anti AI guide.2. Then enter your work email.3. It will ask you to pay or not. Don't pay.4. Wait 2 min. Then open the welcoming email.5. Click on my Notion. Open '.md files'5. Download 'ANTI AI WRITING STYLE .md'. 6. Go to Claude .ai. Don't type your prompt yet.7. Click the '+' button. Upload the anti AI .md first.8. Start every chat with this prompt (copy & paste):"Read my anti-AI writing style file first. It contains every known pattern of AI writing I want to avoid. Apply these as rules to everything you write for me. Do NOT start writing yet - ask me clarifying questions first."By the way, that's how you can tell this is AI-written:1. Stop making it meaningful. 2. The word "is" still exists. Stop writing "serves as."3. Banned words: "underscore, foster, enhance."4. Stop stapling "-ing" phrases to facts. That's fake.5. "Not just X, but also Y" answers nothing.6. Spot the "despite" loop. "Despite success." 7. Name a source, not a list of publications.8. Use a name twice. Not "the artist or figure."9. Calm down with em dashes. Commas still work.10. Check for Markdown leftovers. Asterisks and ##.11. Read it out loud. AI tone means one prompt.12. Details should do something. People still can't tell when AI wrote something.You want to help your network spot it?♻️ Repost this list so they stop falling for it.

full_text_english: Don't use Claude until you read this: (then send it to your entire team)✦ Claude ChatStep 1. Go to claude .ai.Step 2. Type what you need.Step 3. Start here at how-to-claude.aiStep 4. There is no step 5.But here's the mistake:Don't start a new chat every time.Keep going in the same one.Claude gets smarter the more context it has.✦ Claude ProjectsStep 1. Click "Projects" in the sidebar.Step 2. Create a new project.Step 3. Upload your brand voice, docs & references.Step 4. Download my files at how-to-ai.guideStep 5. Don't pay anything, get it in welcome email.It's like handing a new hire a binder on 1st day."Here's how we do things."Every chat after that is informed by that binder.✦ Claude CoworkStep 1. Download the Claude desktop app.Step 2. Click "Cowork" next to Chat.Step 3. Go to this free guide: claude-co.workStep 4. Grant access to your folders.Step 5. Describe the outcome you want.This is the one that replaces your intern.It opens your files. Does the work. Hands you the deliverable. You just review it.✦ Claude SkillsStep 1. Create a SKILL .md file.Step 2. Write out how to do one task perfectly.Step 3. Upload it in Settings → Skills.Step 4. Use Claude Skills: https://lnkd.in/dq6xHS4pStep 5. Claude detects when it's relevant.You don't teach a chef what "cooking" is.You hand them a specific recipe.Skills are recipes.✦ Claude CodeStep 1. Download the Claude desktop app.Step 2. Click the "Code" tab at the top.Step 3. Create a folder on your computer.Step 4. Claude Code: https://lnkd.in/d4qamGtMStep 5. Connect GitHub.You now have a junior developer on call 24/7.You describe the vision. Claude builds it.Pro tip: The biggest mistake across all 5:Describing steps instead of outcomes.Don't say "Open file A, copy column B."Say "Combine the revenue data into one summary."Let Claude figure out the how.To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

full_text_english: Everyone is talking about Claude in 2026. But almost no one has set it up like this:Set-up 1. Create your Voice Profile first✦ Write how you talk. Not how you write.✦ Include: tone, words you never use, & audience.✦ Save it as one .md file. Upload it once.✦ Copy my exact voice file: how-to-ai.guide.Set-up 2. Build your "Claude Cowork" folder✦ One folder on your desktop. Three subfolders.✦ /voice - your tone file and anti-AI writing guide.✦ /context - your offer, audience & examples.✦ /templates - your best past work. The standard.✦ My full folder structure: how-to-ai.guideSet-up 3. Set up Projects before you ever prompt✦ One Project per recurring task. Not per topic.✦ Upload your voice file + 2-3 examples of output.✦ Write custom instructions thoroughly.✦ My Project setup: https://lnkd.in/d8BHqTvVSet-up 4. Stop writing prompts. Start interviewing.✦ Copy-paste my interview prompt✦ From this guide: https://lnkd.in/eF56s4i8✦ It asks you 100 questions about how you think.✦ You answer. The draft writes itself.Set-up 5. Use Claude Dispatch from your phone✦ Text Claude a task. Walk away. Go live your life.✦ It opens Chrome. Posts and messages for you.✦ You come back to updates, not to-do lists.✦ My Dispatch setup: https://lnkd.in/dSaSqRxwSet-up 6. Let Claude build what you describe✦ Say it in English. Claude writes the code.✦ Dashboards. Websites. Internal tools. No coding.✦ Describe it like you'd explain it to a teammate.✦ Claude code guide: https://lnkd.in/gsRhsFxTSet-up 7. Review like a boss. Not like a user.✦ Read v1. Name every single wrong thing.✦ Push back: "This sounds like AI. Rewrite [part]"✦ If you can't spot, you're not ready to delegate it.The gap in 2026 is who set it up to work for them.Everyone else is still typing into a blank chat box.Help others be better at Claude ♻️ repost this.PS: I write a newsletter to 436,000+ readers.↳ Join us for free at how-to-ai.guide.↳ Open the welcome email after subscring.↳ Download all of my infographics, also for free.

hook_text_english: 32 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.Example: ELI5: [your topic] → get a simple, kid-friendly explanation.——/ELI5 is us
full_text_english: 32 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.Example: ELI5: [your topic] → get a simple, kid-friendly explanation.——/ELI5 is used to explain as if to a 5-year-old./TLDL summarizes a very long text in a few lines./STEP-BY-STEP lays out reasoning step by step./CHECKLIST turns a response into a checklist./EXEC SUMMARY gives a quick executive-style summary./ACT AS makes ChatGPT speak in a specific role./BRIEFLY forces a very short answer./JARGON asks to use technical vocabulary./AUDIENCE adapts the response to a chosen audience./TONE changes the tone (formal, funny, dramatic, etc.)./DEV MODE simulates a raw, technical developer style./PM MODE gives a project-management perspective./SWOT produces a strengths/weaknesses/opportunities/threats analysis./FORMAT AS enforces a specific format (table, JSON, etc.)./COMPARE puts two or more things side by side./MULTI-PERSPECTIVE shows several points of view./CONTEXT STACK keeps multiple layers of context in memory./BEGIN WITH / END WITH forces starting or ending with something./ROLE: TASK: FORMAT: explicitly defines the role, the task, and the expected format./SCHEMA generates a structured outline or a data model./REWRITE AS: rephrases in a requested style./REFLECTIVE MODE prompts the AI to reflect on its own answer./SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK asks to identify biases./DELIBERATE THINKING forces slower, more thoughtful reasoning./NO AUTOPILOT forbids superficial, autopilot responses./EVAL-SELF asks for a critical self-evaluation of the response./PARALLEL LENSES examines from several angles in parallel./FIRST PRINCIPLES rebuilds from fundamental basics./CHAIN OF THOUGHT shows intermediate reasoning./PITFALLS identifies possible traps and errors./METRICS MODE expresses answers with measures and indicators./GUARDRAIL sets strict boundaries not to cross.——After months of testing Claude, I built a single prompt library with every prompt I personally use.To access it, complete these 4 steps:1. Subscribe (for free) → how-to-ai.guide.2. Open my welcome email.3. Hit the automatic reply button inside.4. Receive your prompt library + bonus video.
